signals.title: USD/CAD — Inflation Surprise + Oil + Carry
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3712 following a hot US CPI report and Trump's oil export headlines. Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25% provides a +150bp carry advantage for USD, while the bear steepener regime (US 2s10s +55.3bp) reinforces USD dominance. CAD COT LF net short -46,889 (26.4% long) reflects heavy institutional selling. Strong US inflation data offsets WTI Oil's +3.35% rally, keeping the pair bid. Watch for a sustained break above 1.3750 to confirm trend extension.

signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 113.886 as the G8 spread between leader (AUD) and laggard (JPY) hits 0.849%. RBA 4.35% vs BoJ 0.75% creates a +360bp carry premium, the widest in the G8 complex, supporting AUD despite the US inflation shock. AUD COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long) shows crowded institutional positioning, while retail is 84% short, creating a sharp divergence. Carry remains the primary driver while VIX remains at 18.7 (normal volatility).

signals.title: EUR/USD — Technical Break + Correlation Shift
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.17380 testing the 200-hour moving average (1.17367) following the US CPI beat. EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.705 (z=+1.65σ) indicates EUR is increasingly trading as a risk asset rather than on rate differentials alone. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (+175bp USD favor) structurally limits upside. EUR COT LF net short -21,011 suggests specs are positioned for further downside. Watch for a daily close below 1.1730 to confirm the short-term bias shift.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5944 with COT LF net short -19,294 (18.5% long) while the RBNZ maintains a hawkish market bias. If the RBNZ signals a hike at the 27 May meeting, the crowded spec short position faces significant unwind risk. Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% (+150bp USD favor) currently protects the downside, but the institutional short-interest extreme makes the pair sensitive to any hawkish NZD surprises.
